passage.title: Charity Walk Checkpoint Redesign
passage.text: A charity walk team hoped younger visitors could follow the event path without getting lost. At first, the group used tall posters filled with many small arrows, but the arrows were hard to match with the real path. A practice group of children said "The names are clear, yet I cannot tell where the next game is." After hearing that, the team matched each activity zone with one shape and added a symbol line that repeated from sign to sign. They also added helper signs before each turn so families saw the next step before reaching a crowded corner. On the next test day, the practice group followed the event path with fewer stops. The team noticed that repeated symbols made the route easier to remember. Because of that result, the event group chose to update the rest of the festival path in the same way.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Painting a room with brighter colors
Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Redesigning festival directions for younger visitors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: The first plan had too many steps
Students could not tell what to do next
The arrows were hard to match with the real path
The labels were too small to read quickly
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the group add before each turn?
choices: Color blocks
Helper signs
Larger arrows
Short signs
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does volunteers mean in the sixth sentence?
choices: People who help without being paid
A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It made the room much larger
It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
The repeated shapes made the event path easier to follow
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Remove every sign from the room
Ask students to memorize everything
Update the rest of the festival path with the new system
Use the same clearer style in more places
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
